answer: GitHub will terminate that User’s Account immediately.

GitHub considers the contents of private repositories to be confidential to you. GitHub will protect the contents of private repositories from unauthorized use, access, or disclosure in the same manner that it would use to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature and in no event with less than a reasonable degree of care. GitHub personnel may only access the content of your private repositories in the situations described in the Privacy Statement. You may choose to enable additional access to your private repositories for certain services or features, and GitHub may be compelled by law to disclose the contents. GitHub will provide notice regarding access to private repository content unless for legal disclosure, to comply with legal obligations, or where otherwise bound by law, for automated scanning, or in response to a security threat or other risk to security.

You will promptly notify GitHub by contacting us through the GitHub Support portal if you become aware of any unauthorized use of, or access to, our Service through your Account, including any unauthorized use of your password or Account.

GitHub has the right to store, archive, parse, and display User-Generated Content, and make incidental copies as necessary to provide the Service, including improving the Service over time. This includes copying it to their database, making backups, showing it to you and other users, parsing it into a search index or otherwise analyzing it on their servers, sharing it with other users, and performing it if the content is something like music or video. GitHub does not have the right to sell User-Generated Content or otherwise distribute or use it outside of providing the Service, except as part of archiving programs like the GitHub Arctic Code Vault and GitHub Archive Program. GitHub also has the right to refuse or remove any User-Generated Content that, in their sole discretion, violates any laws or GitHub terms or policies.

It is your responsibility to properly cancel your Account with GitHub. You can cancel your Account at any time by going into your Settings in the global navigation bar at the top of the screen. The Account screen provides a simple, no questions asked cancellation link. GitHub is not able to cancel Accounts in response to an email or phone request. GitHub will retain and use your information as necessary to comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce agreements, but barring legal requirements, will delete your full profile and the Content of your repositories within 90 days of cancellation or termination (though some information may remain in encrypted backups). This information cannot be recovered once your Account is canceled. Content contributed to other Users' repositories or that other Users have forked will not be deleted. Upon request, GitHub will make a reasonable effort to provide an Account owner with a copy of lawful, non-infringing Account contents after cancellation, termination, or downgrade, if requested within 90 days.
